title: Breaking Up Is Hard To Do 2

jokes.text: Sounds like somebody got a little booty duty, huh, Uncle Phil?
jokes.setup: Phil mentions things they've been putting off since the trial ended
jokes.punchline: Will turns it into a sexual innuendo about 'booty duty'
jokes.explanation: Classic Will inappropriate comment. Decent wordplay but fairly predictable sexual humor

jokes.text: After which, all the models are gonna wanna come home with Ashley and have coffee.
jokes.setup: Ashley's modeling opportunity discussion
jokes.punchline: Will's transparent attempt to use Ashley's modeling to meet women
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's opportunistic nature, well-integrated into the scene

jokes.text: Geoffrey, start making some damn coffee.
jokes.setup: Will's fantasy about models coming over
jokes.punchline: He immediately orders Geoffrey to prepare, showing his presumption
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character work - Will's entitlement and confidence perfectly captured

jokes.text: I'll alert the fish.
jokes.setup: Phil says Joe Winters is on thin ice
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry literal interpretation about alerting fish under ice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan wit. Excellent wordplay on the idiom with flawless economy and character voice

jokes.text: Oh, great. On top of it all, now I'm an orphan?
jokes.setup: Being told her parents broke up
jokes.punchline: Hilary's self-centered reaction focusing on her 'orphan' status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary self-absorption. The narcissistic 'orphan' concern is pitch-perfect characterization

jokes.text: Hilary, you're 27. / I know. No one adopts anyone over 9.
jokes.setup: Carlton points out Hilary is 27 years old
jokes.punchline: Hilary's concern about adoption age limits
jokes.explanation: Great setup-punchline structure with perfect Hilary logic. Clean execution of her ditzy character

jokes.text: Hey, Uncle Phil... I just want you to know that it's okay for a man to cry.
jokes.setup: Phil trying to act like everything's fine
jokes.punchline: Will's awkward attempt at emotional support
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Will's well-meaning but clumsy emotional intelligence

jokes.text: I'm on your team, Governor Banks.
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming he's not concerned about parents' fight
jokes.punchline: Immediately shows loyalty to Phil with political title
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton suck-up behavior, using the political title shows his calculated loyalty

jokes.text: No one boils water like you, Geoffrey.
jokes.setup: Phil asking what's cooking, Geoffrey says boiling water
jokes.punchline: Phil's desperate attempt to make normal conversation with ridiculous praise
jokes.explanation: Excellent portrayal of Phil's emotional state through absurd over-praise of basic tasks

jokes.text: Boy, there are all kinds of ways to keep in touch.
jokes.setup: Phil rambling about modern communications and mail
jokes.punchline: Transparent fishing for Vivian to call him
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Phil's desperate hope for contact, though somewhat drawn out

jokes.text: Your skirt steaks are down at the depot. They'll be here by morning.
jokes.setup: Phil desperately asking about calls after Geoffrey says there was one
jokes.punchline: The call was just about meat delivery
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure. Geoffrey's deadpan delivery amplifies Phil's disappointment

jokes.text: That's not funny. / You are so right.
jokes.setup: Will makes joke about single life, Phil says it's not funny
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately agrees with Phil
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton brown-nosing captured in minimal words. Great economy of character comedy

jokes.text: Well, she said, 'Did you talk to your mom back in Phil-adelphia?'
jokes.setup: Phil asking what Vivian said about him specifically
jokes.punchline: Will's pun combining Phil's name with Philadelphia
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - shows Will's wit and Phil's desperation

jokes.text: Besides, they're in her camp.
jokes.setup: Carlton advises Phil not to go get Vivian as it weakens his position
jokes.punchline: Treating family dynamics like political strategy/warfare
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton character work - applying political thinking to marriage, missing emotional point

jokes.text: Yeah, you know, I been thinking about leaving. / Leave.
jokes.setup: Will says he's been unhappy and thinking of leaving too
jokes.punchline: Phil's immediate, blunt response
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy. Phil's blunt dismissal is both harsh and hilarious

jokes.text: Child, please. Since Michael Jordan went back to the NBA... and started wearing them shorts again. Ah! Look at those buns. Booyah!
jokes.setup: Vivian asking when Helen became a sports enthusiast
jokes.punchline: Helen's sexual objectification of Michael Jordan
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate humor for Helen but relies on basic sexual objectification

jokes.text: I'd give him a blast of pepper spray but I'm afraid he'd just eat it.
jokes.setup: Phil is at the door wanting to come in
jokes.punchline: Helen's joke about Phil eating pepper spray due to his appetite
jokes.explanation: Sharp character humor targeting Phil's weight with creative absurdist twist on pepper spray

jokes.text: Yeah. She's teaching me all kinds of cool stuff, fat-head.
jokes.setup: Phil asking Nicky how he's doing at Aunt Helen's
jokes.punchline: Nicky casually calling Phil 'fat-head' as learned behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Helen's influence, funny contrast between innocent child and insult

jokes.text: Get it appraised!
jokes.setup: Phil gives Vivian a Tiffany's gift
jokes.punchline: Helen's cynical advice shouted from offscreen
jokes.explanation: Perfect Helen character moment with great economy - cynical but practical advice

jokes.text: I'm moving closer to the door, Vivian. This is your last chance. I mean it.
jokes.setup: Phil's dramatic threat to leave
jokes.punchline: His pathetic play-by-play of moving toward the door
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy concept - the desperate play-by-play makes Phil's bluff pathetic and hilarious

jokes.text: Oh, for God's sake, would you leave already!
jokes.setup: Phil's extended dramatic exit threat
jokes.punchline: Vivian's exasperated demand he actually follow through
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Phil's extended bluff, Vivian's exasperation is relatable and funny

jokes.text: Hil, that's not a movie, that's what happened. / Oh, well, someone should make a movie of it.
jokes.setup: Hilary describes their parents' fight as 'like in that movie'
jokes.punchline: When told it's real life, she suggests making a movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary logic - her inability to distinguish reality from entertainment, then monetizing it

jokes.text: Don't worry, Big Guy, I got you covered. He was with me the whole time.
jokes.setup: Kids asking what Phil did to make Vivian not come back
jokes.punchline: Will providing Phil an alibi like he's covering a crime
jokes.explanation: Good Will loyalty moment, treating marriage dispute like a criminal matter is absurd and funny

jokes.text: Once upon a time a little girl could've been a supermodel... but her parents got divorced and she lost it all. The end. Wanna hear another?
jokes.setup: Nicky asking for a story
jokes.punchline: Ashley's bitter fairy tale about her ruined modeling career
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on fairy tales, good character work showing Ashley's self-pity and dramatic nature

jokes.text: Exercise would do you good. / Was that a fat joke?
jokes.setup: Ashley saying she has to walk to work, Phil suggests exercise
jokes.punchline: Phil's paranoid interpretation as a weight insult
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's sensitivity about his weight and emotional state, decent setup-punchline

jokes.text: For the love of God, man, shred, shred, shred.
jokes.setup: Phil has Geoffrey doing various tasks while shredding documents
jokes.punchline: His desperate, manic insistence on continued shredding
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Phil's paranoid political mindset, manic energy is funny

jokes.text: I have no recollection of the event in question.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking about Phil's involvement in political scheming
jokes.punchline: Phil's repeated political non-denial denial phrase
jokes.explanation: Good political satire, Phil using formal political language with his own son is absurd

jokes.text: Now, be gone.
jokes.setup: A man compliments Hilary despite her parents' divorce
jokes.punchline: Her imperious dismissal after accepting the compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary entitlement - accepting praise then dismissing the person with royal authority

jokes.text: Hey, what up, dawg? What it be like?
jokes.setup: Phil interrupting Will's proposal to act cool
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at street slang
jokes.explanation: Classic Phil trying to be cool moment, though the awkward slang is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: How about I shower with you? / Hey, hey, hey, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Phil suggesting he go shower because he feels dirty
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate suggestion to shower together with Katie
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy showing Phil's inappropriate behavior while separated, relies on shock value

jokes.text: Every time! Every damn time. Every time!
jokes.setup: Phil has ruined another important moment
jokes.punchline: Will's mounting frustration expressed through repetition
jokes.explanation: Good escalating frustration, though the repetition could be more economical

jokes.text: He's butchering it.
jokes.setup: Carlton singing 'Girl from Ipanema' badly
jokes.punchline: Vivian's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction humor, economical but not particularly creative

jokes.text: they got three pages worth of pork dishes up in here.
jokes.setup: Will trying to convince Phil to stay at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Appeals to Phil's appetite with specific pork menu details
jokes.explanation: Good character knowledge - Will knowing exactly how to motivate Phil through food

jokes.text: They wouldn't let me bring my leaf blower.
jokes.setup: Phil commenting on the restaurant's atmosphere
jokes.punchline: His bizarre non-sequitur about being denied a leaf blower
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random and absurd line that's both unexpected and quotable

jokes.text: I don't hear nothing.
jokes.setup: Will trapping Phil and Vivian together to force them to talk
jokes.punchline: His theatrical declaration while presumably covering his ears
jokes.explanation: Good Will scheming moment, theatrical presentation of forced mediation

jokes.text: You gonna bring my Aunt Viv back
jokes.setup: Will continuing to sing while they argue
jokes.punchline: Making their reconciliation part of his song lyrics
jokes.explanation: Continued creative karaoke intervention, personalizing classic songs

jokes.text: They won't. Just because I'm running for political office doesn't mean I'm gonna win. / Philip, you win at everything. / Ha, ha. Touché, you got me there.
jokes.setup: Phil arguing the political demands won't last forever
jokes.punchline: Vivian pointing out Phil's track record, Phil's acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Phil's success being his downfall, good self-awareness

jokes.text: What am I gonna do with all those campaign buttons?
jokes.setup: Phil's emotional decision to choose family over politics
jokes.punchline: His practical concern about leftover campaign materials
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic moment with mundane concern, very Phil-like practicality
